issues.description: AC #3 says `nemo status --json | jq '.loops[0].loop_id'` returns a UUID, but the existing implementation (status.rs:33) outputs `resp.loops` as a bare JSON array, not a `{"loops": [...]}` wrapper. The correct jq expression for the current schema is `jq '.[0].loop_id'`. Since FR-3a and NFR-1 both say to preserve the existing output schema, the AC is wrong, not the code.
issues.suggestion: Change AC #3 to `nemo status --json | jq '.[0].loop_id'` to match the actual existing schema, or explicitly document that the schema is changing (which would violate NFR-1).

issues.description: FR-3a adds `--json` to approve, cancel, resume, extend, models, and auth, describing them as 'structured response object' or 'structured response', but no JSON schemas are defined for any of these commands. FR-3b says 'JSON output schema documented in nemo help ai' but FR-1's JSON schema only covers the mega-primer content, not per-command output schemas. Implementor must guess the field names and structure.
issues.suggestion: Define the JSON output schema for each new `--json` command (at minimum: approve, cancel, resume, extend, models, auth), similar to how the status and cache schemas are already documented by the existing code. Even a brief example per command would suffice.

issues.description: The spec uses `--format=json` on the help subcommand (FR-1c, FR-5c) but `--json` boolean flags on stateful commands (FR-3a). This dual convention is never explained and could confuse implementors. Additionally, `--format` implies future extensibility (yaml, toml?) but only `json` is defined.
issues.suggestion: Either unify on `--json` everywhere, or add a brief note explaining why the help subcommand uses `--format` (e.g., because the default output is Markdown prose, not structured data, so a format selector is more appropriate than a boolean toggle). Specify that `--format` only accepts `json` in v1.

issues.description: FR-2c says long_about should be applied to 'cache (once shipped)' but cache is a parent command with a subcommand (`cache show`). The spec doesn't clarify whether long_about goes on the `Cache` parent, the `CacheAction::Show` subcommand, or both.
issues.suggestion: Specify: apply long_about to `cache show` (the actionable subcommand). The `cache` parent already has clap's auto-generated subcommand listing.

issues.description: NFR-1 says 'All existing invocations produce identical stdout/stderr', but adding `capabilities` as a new top-level subcommand and replacing the built-in `help` with a custom subcommand will change the output of `nemo --help` (the subcommand listing). This is a minor contradiction.
issues.suggestion: Weaken NFR-1 to: 'All existing command invocations produce identical stdout/stderr, except that `nemo --help` will list additional subcommands (capabilities, help with new flags).'

issues.description: The spec doesn't define what happens when conflicting or nonsensical flag combinations are used on the help subcommand. For example: `nemo help --all ai` (positional arg + --all), `nemo help --format=json` (no subcommand, no --all), or `nemo help ai --all`. These edge cases need defined behavior.
issues.suggestion: Add a brief interaction table: `help --all ai` → error ('--all and a specific command are mutually exclusive'), `help --format=json` without ai or --all → error or JSON version of the subcommand listing, `help ai --all` → error.

issues.description: FR-6b's capabilities JSON lists 18 commands including 'help' but not 'capabilities' itself. After implementation, there will be 19 subcommands (17 existing + help as explicit + capabilities). The commands array should be self-consistent.
issues.suggestion: Add 'capabilities' to the example commands array in FR-6b, and note that the array is auto-derived from clap subcommands so it will always be accurate.

issues.description: FR-1a says 'also nemo help llm, alias' but doesn't specify the mechanism. Is 'llm' a clap alias on the positional argument? A separate positional value matched in code? A clap visible_alias on a subcommand? The implementation approach affects how it appears in help text.
issues.suggestion: Specify: implement as a second accepted value for the positional argument (e.g., match 'ai' | 'llm' in the handler), not as a clap alias, to keep the help output clean.

issues.description: The Baseline section says 'flat list of 17 commands' but after this spec ships there will be 18-19 (adding capabilities, and help becomes explicit). The spec should acknowledge this count change.
issues.suggestion: Minor wording fix — note that the spec adds capabilities as a new top-level command.

issues.description: FR-4a's error hint table includes 'Claude token expired' and 'Spec not found' but doesn't specify what HTTP status codes accompany these. The matching rules section says 'combine substring matching with HTTP status code' but only the 409 case is explicitly paired.
issues.suggestion: Add expected HTTP status codes for each error pattern in the table (e.g., 401 for token expired, 404 for spec not found) to make matching more robust.
